title: The Water Cycle
transcript: Today we will discuss the water cycle, also known as the hydrologic cycle. The water cycle describes the continuous movement of water on, above, and below the surface of the Earth. Although the balance of water on Earth remains fairly constant over time, individual water molecules can come and go in a hurry. The cycle involves several key processes: evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection. Evaporation occurs when the sun heats water in rivers, lakes, or oceans and turns it into vapor or steam. This vapor rises into the air. Condensation happens when water vapor in the air cools down and changes back into liquid, forming clouds. Precipitation occurs when so much water has condensed that the air cannot hold it anymore. The clouds get heavy and water falls back to the Earth in the form of rain, snow, sleet, or hail. Finally, collection occurs when the water falls back to Earth and accumulates in oceans, rivers, lakes, or streams. Some of it soaks into the ground and becomes groundwater. This cycle then repeats continuously.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: The water cycle and its key processes
The causes of climate change
The formation of ocean currents
The history of Earth science
questions.explanation: The lecture introduces the water cycle and explains its main processes.

options: The sun heats water and turns it into vapor
Water vapor cools and forms clouds
Water falls back to Earth as rain or snow
Water accumulates in rivers and oceans
questions.explanation: Evaporation occurs when the sun heats water and turns it into vapor or steam.

questions.stem: According to the lecture, what causes precipitation?
options: The air can no longer hold the condensed water
The sun stops heating the ocean
Groundwater rises to the surface
Clouds move closer to the mountains
questions.explanation: Precipitation occurs when so much water has condensed that the air cannot hold it anymore.

questions.stem: Where does some of the collected water go?
options: It soaks into the ground and becomes groundwater
It evaporates immediately
It turns into salt water
It freezes into glaciers
questions.explanation: The lecture states that some water soaks into the ground and becomes groundwater.

questions.stem: What does the professor imply about individual water molecules?
options: They move quickly through the cycle
They remain in the same location forever
They only exist in the atmosphere
They are not affected by temperature
questions.explanation: The professor says individual water molecules can come and go in a hurry, implying they move quickly.
